description:
You are the release manager for a small project.  
A fresh build of version 1.0 is sitting in  

/home/user/releases/v1.0/  

with the exact **three** items shown below (nothing else is inside that directory):

1. README.md  
2. bin/start.sh  
3. config/settings.conf  

Your job is to hand-off a compressed deployment bundle to the operations team and, at the same time, prove that the archive really contains the expected files.

What you must do:

1. Create a **gzip-compressed tar archive** named  
   /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0.tar.gz  
   that contains the complete v1.0 directory **and preserves its internal directory structure exactly as is**.
2. Immediately after creating the archive, produce a *verification log* that lists the archived paths **exactly** as the command “tar -tzf /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0.tar.gz” would print them.  
   • Save this plain-text list to  
     /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0_contents.log  
   • The file must contain precisely three lines, each line terminated by a single UNIX newline (`\n`).  
     The required line order and spelling are:  
       v1.0/README.md  
       v1.0/bin/start.sh  
       v1.0/config/settings.conf

Important verification details the automated test will check:

• /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
• /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0_contents.log exists, is world-readable, and contains **exactly** the three lines (and only those three lines) shown above.  
• The paths stored inside the archive match the list in the log file byte-for-byte.

Accomplish everything with one concise shell line if you like (a single compound command is fine). No super-user privileges are required.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/releases/v1.0/ exists and contains:
- /home/user/releases/v1.0/README.md  
  Content: “Project v1.0 — release notes.” (one short line, ending with newline)
- /home/user/releases/v1.0/bin/ (directory)  
  - /home/user/releases/v1.0/bin/start.sh  
    Content: “#!/usr/bin/env bash\necho 'Starting v1.0'\n”
- /home/user/releases/v1.0/config/ (directory)  
  - /home/user/releases/v1.0/config/settings.conf  
    Content: “mode=production\nversion=1.0\n”

Directory /home/user/releases_packages/ is present and writable by the user but empty.

Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0.tar.gz is created.  
   Running “tar -tzf /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0.tar.gz” outputs exactly:  
     v1.0/README.md  
     v1.0/bin/start.sh  
     v1.0/config/settings.conf  
   (each terminated with ‘\n’, no extra blank lines).

2. /home/user/releases_packages/v1.0_contents.log exists with 3 lines exactly as above (byte-perfect match, newline-terminated, no trailing spaces, no extra newline at the end).

Permissions: all new files should be 644 and belong to user:user.

The automated test will:
a) verify the archive’s presence and integrity via “tar -tzf”.  
b) compare the tar output with the contents of v1.0_contents.log.  
c) ensure no additional files exist inside /home/user/releases_packages/ beyond the two mentioned.
name: task_000000_b57c32d1
